_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to create different types of texts — written and spoken — that reflect on challenging and complex issues, combining different modes and using sophisticated language choices.

_source.w: We are learning to create different types of texts — written and spoken — that reflect on challenging and complex issues, combining different modes and using sophisticated language choices.
y: Creating texts that engage with complex issues demonstrates intellectual maturity and communicative skill.
Combining modes — written, spoken, visual, digital — reflects how sophisticated communication works in the real world.
These are the culminating writing skills that prepare us for post-secondary academic and professional demands.
eg: We write an extended analytical essay on a complex issue, demonstrating control over content, structure and language.
We create a multimodal text that combines written, visual and audio elements to address a challenging topic.
We deliver a spoken text that engages with a complex issue and demonstrates sophisticated reasoning.
We select language features deliberately to achieve specific effects suited to our purpose and audience.
We create texts that reflect multiple perspectives on an issue rather than simplifying to a single view.
We evaluate our own texts for the sophistication and effectiveness of our language and structural choices.
